title: polystichum squarrosum
intro: Polystichum squarrosum, also known as the creeping shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. The creeping shield fern has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Polystichum squarrosum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of creams or salves.
